preamble: R-vocalization into a palatal glide occurs in various dialects and speech stages, reflecting a reduction in the articulatory effort of the rhotic and its transition into a semivowel.

examples.note: In certain rural Brazilian Portuguese dialects, the coda /r/ vocalizes to a palatal glide /j/.
A common phonological substitution in child language development where the complex rhotic is replaced by the simpler palatal glide.

One of the most consistent sound changes in Mayan historical phonology: Proto-Mayan *r shifted to /j/ in the western branches including Chujean-Q'anjob'alan, Cholan-Tseltalan, Yukatek, and Huastecan. Eastern branches (K'ichean-Mamean) retain the original *r, providing the comparative basis for reconstruction.

Attested in Ch'ol, Tzeltal, Yucatec Maya, and Tojolab'al. The change is exceptionless within each affected branch and serves as one of the primary isoglosses distinguishing western from eastern Mayan.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Law, D. (2013). Mayan Historical Linguistics in a New Age. Language and Linguistics Compass.
